summary: Validated SSE real-time notifications + B2B tenant Welcome/Invite flow end-to-end. All 16 backend pytest cases passed (SSE stream, admin publish, admin session-action guards, invite flow, mock emails). Frontend WelcomePage + sidebar entry + invite form + email preview modal all working. End-to-end SSE delivery verified via backend test (admin publishes -> SSE reader receives within ~1s) AND via UI (invite triggers real-time 'Invitación enviada' toast to inviter).

issues: Uses native HTML <select> for role instead of shadcn Select component — inconsistent with rest of app but functional. Minor UX inconsistency only.

action_items: (Optional) Replace native <select> in invite-role-select with shadcn Select for UI consistency
(Future) Wire up invite_token activation flow so invited users can set password via link (currently generates token but no activation endpoint)
critical_code_review_comments: SSE auth via query-param token is acceptable since EventSource cannot set headers, but token will appear in access logs — document this trade-off or switch to short-lived signed tokens for SSE in a future iteration.
notifications_test endpoint accepts arbitrary user_id without verifying the target exists; could publish events to queues that don't exist (harmless but log-noisy).

admin_session_action: the early branch `if tsplus_sid: ...` means sessions with no tsplus_sid and no guac_conn_id still return success=True without actually doing anything — could be misleading. Consider returning success=False or 'no-op' indicator.
invite_users response returns `user_id` for already_exists case (OK), but does not include `email_id` on existing users; that's fine since no email is sent. Works as expected.

Mock email body_html contains welcome_message unescaped — small XSS surface if admin is compromised, but since iframe is sandbox='' in the preview, the risk is contained. Consider HTML-escaping body.welcome_message before interpolation.
test_credentials: admin@windesk.cloud / Admin123! (admin) and usuario1@windesk.cloud / Demo123! (user). Login returns { access_token, token_type, user } — NOT { token } (key name is access_token).
seed_data_creation: Test invitees with unique `fe_test_{marker}@example.com` and `test_invitee_{uuid}@example.com` prefixes created in db.users + db.mock_emails. Not cleaned up; harmless since they have no password. Main agent may want a cleanup script that deletes users matching ^(fe_test_|test_invitee_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login response key is `access_token` (not `token`). For SSE tests in pytest, use requests.get(..., stream=True) and iter_content; the first chunk always contains `event: ready`. When Playwright navigates to a page with an open SSE EventSource, use wait_until='domcontentloaded' (not 'networkidle') because the SSE keep-alive prevents networkidle from ever firing. New endpoints: /api/notifications/stream, /api/notifications/test, /api/admin/sessions/{id}/action, /api/admin/emails[/id], /api/tenants/invite-users, /api/tenants/invited-users. All admin-only except the SSE stream (which is auth'd by token query param).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. One test-infra adjustment: initial _login() helper assumed response key 'token' but actual key is 'access_token'; fixed in the new test file.
